summary: deployment-key-auditor exfiltrates host info and environment to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: Package ships an install/lifecycle hook in package.json (line 31) that uses child_process, os, and https to collect host identifiers (os.hostname(), whoami, process.cwd()) and environment data and POST them to https://ddjidd564.github.io — a GitHub Pages site under an unrelated personal account, used here as a beaconing endpoint. scanner.js corroborates the attack: it imports fs/os/https/child_process, calls execSync and os.hostname() to gather system intel (lines 6-9, 233-246), checks for sensitive files via fs.existsSync (lines 93, 114), issues https.request/https.get POSTs (lines 142-161) to the same hardcoded ddjidd564.github.io endpoint, and shells out to curl (line 324) against that host. The combination of hardcoded non-publisher C2, host/credential-path enumeration, and POST of system data is the canonical exfiltration fingerprint and provides direct attacker benefit on every install/run. The 'deployment-key-auditor' name suggests it is positioned to be installed in CI/CD or key-management contexts where harvested data is especially sensitive.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
